summary: Stellar Scarlet Begonias, tight second set, vibrant Brent keys
blurb: Highlights include a soaring Scarlet Begonias > Fire on the Mountain sequence and an inspired Eyes of the World. Jerry's lead work shines while Phil lays down a thunderous bass groove. The second set builds momentum with strong drums and Brent’s lively keyboards.
review: On September 5, 1985 the Dead took advantage of a sunny Red Rocks afternoon to deliver a relaxed yet adventurous performance. The first set meandered through a rare Candyman, a tight Peggy‑O and a soulful Let It Grow, but it was the second set that truly lifted off – a blistering Scarlet Begonias that segued into Fire on the Mountain, followed by an expansive Estimated Prophet → Eyes of the World > Drums > Space suite. Jerry Garcia turned the Scarlet solo into a masterclass of transpositions and reverse phrasing, and his improvisations stayed on‑game through Eyes of the World. Phil Lesh’s bass was described as “hemorrhaging bass farts,” driving the band with gusto, while Brent Mydland kept the harmonic landscape bright, even slipping in an odd post‑Cold Rain keyboard tease that added a quirky charm. The drum duo of Mickey Hart and Bill Kreutzmann delivered one of the show’s high points – a headphone‑friendly Drums segment that many fans rank among the top‑20 all time. Though a few moments (notably Estimated Prophet) felt a little loose, the overall vibe was tight, memorable, and quintessentially ‘80s Dead.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Multiple recordings praise the show with positive language (e.g., "outstanding solo," "solid second‑set gems," "great keyboard work"). The majority sentiment is positive (3 positive, 2 mixed) and consistent highlights like Scarlet Begonias, Eyes of the World, and strong rhythm section are noted across sources. Mixed comments mention minor flaws (slow SBD, Estimated Prophet flub) but do not outweigh the overall enthusiasm, warranting a moderate positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Clear soundboard mix with pleasant echoes, captures the highlight songs well despite slight slowdown; considered the definitive source by listeners.

key_highlights: Scarlet Begonias > Fire on the Mountain sequence shines with a soaring solo.
Eyes of the World features an on‑game Jerry Garcia improvisation.
Brent Mydland’s vibrant keyboard textures and quirky post‑Cold Rain tease.
Bill Kreutzmann and Mickey Hart deliver a top‑20‑ranked Drums segment.

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers a fiery, adventurous lead throughout, with an outstanding solo on Scarlet Begonias and inspired phrasing during Eyes of the World; occasional messiness noted but overall captivating.
band_performance.Phil: Provides a thunderous, driving bass foundation, highlighted by vivid “hemorrhaging bass farts” on Let It Grow and solid support across the set.
band_performance.Bob: Adds tasteful slide work early in the show and contributes rhythm guitar throughout, though his Estimated Prophet solo shows a brief stumble.
band_performance.Brent: Offers lively, melodic keyboard work that shines on Big River and adds an unusual post‑Cold Rain intro; his textures keep the harmonic flow bright.
band_performance.Billy: Partners with Mickey to create a standout Drums segment praised as headphone‑friendly and among the band’s best.
